user_query:
I keep a tiny Python tool to plan jazz setlists with my son, and I want it cleaned up before our Sunday rehearsal. Please do a focused code review and refactor, then prep meeting notes for us.

Scope and inputs are all local:
- Code: src/setlist.py, src/utils.py
- Config: config/settings.yaml
- Data: data/songs.csv

Refactor and config requirements:
1) Remove the duplicated CSV loader so there is exactly one load_songs function defined in src/utils.py. Update src/setlist.py to import and use utils.load_songs.
2) Standardize tempo units to bpm across the code. Ensure the loader accepts a CSV column named either "tempo" or "bpm" but always exposes a numeric dictionary key "bpm" (int) for downstream use. Update config/settings.yaml so tempo_unit: bpm (lowercase).
3) Fix the set duration calculation: use the CSV's duration_min column directly for totals in minutes (do not convert from seconds). Keep existing functionality otherwise and add clear type hints and succinct docstrings to public functions you touch.

Deliverables and output paths:
- Overwrite the refactored Python files in place at src/setlist.py and src/utils.py.
- Overwrite the updated config at config/settings.yaml with tempo_unit set to bpm.
- Create docs/meeting_notes_father_son.md with these sections and minimum content:
  • Agenda (short bullets)
  • Codebase Findings (bullets must mention: the removed duplicate loader, the bpm unit standardization, and the duration calculation fix)
  • Practice Focus (derived from data/songs.csv):
    - A ranked top-3 list by highest tempo, showing each song's title and bpm value.
    - A list of any songs with missing key or tempo/bpm, noting which field is missing for each.
  • Action Items (at least 4 bullets, each ending with an owner in parentheses: Dad or Son). Include at least two code follow-ups and two rehearsal-focused tasks referencing songs from the Practice Focus.

Notes:
- Do not change data/songs.csv; normalize in code instead.
- Keep the code runnable with only the provided files.
- Ensure there is no second definition of load_songs in src/setlist.py after refactor.

Thanks—this will help us align the code and our practice plan before we hit the shed together. All file paths are relative to the workspace directory.
